P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 11 00 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 9581.4 
Technical Assistance 
•• 
Administratian 
(916) 322-5662 
322·5660 
Ricardo Ramirez 
806 Hudson Street 
Davis, CA 
95616 
Dear Mr. Ramirez: 
Executive/Legal 
322-5901 
Enforcement 
322-6«1 
September 12, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice, Our 
Advice No. A-83-203 
You have asked whether, as a former official of the 
Department of Parks and Recreation, you may enter into a 
contract with the Department in connection with a proposal to 
promote access to Department facilities for the Hispanic 
Community. 
When you worked for the Department, you were engaged 
in planning activities designed to promote minority group access 
to Department facilities. 
The Political Reform Act, Government Code Sections 87400-
87405,1/ prohibits a former state official from representing 
any person, for pay, before his former agency, in connection 
with any specific proceeding or specific matter in which he 
participated as a state official. 
The type of proceeding or 
matter covered by this section is defined in Section 87400(c) : 
(c) 
"Judicial, quasi-judicial or other pro-
ceeding" means any proceeding, application, request for 
a ruling or other determination, contract, claim, 
controversy,' investigation, charge, accusation, arrest 
or other particular matter involving a specific party 
or parties in any court or state administrative agency, 
including but not limited to any proceeding governed by 
Chapter 5 (commencing with Section 11500) of Division 3 
of Title 2 of the Government Code. 
From my conversation with you on Septmeber 8, 1983, it does 
not appear that, while working for the Department, you were 
1/ All statutory references made are to the Government 
Code, unless otherwise stated. 


Ricardo Ramirez 
September 12, 1983 
Page Two 
involved in specific proceedings of the type covered by Section 
87400. 
You stated that you were involved in general planning 
and policy for the Department; you were not involved in the 
development of individual projects involving specific sites, 
specific individuals or organizations, specific facilities, or 
specific programs. 
So long as you will not be paid for repre-
senting any individual or organization in connection with a 
particular project or proceeding in which you participated as a 
public official, Sections 87400-87405 are not applicable. 
The 
fact that your proposed activities may involve the same general 
policy considerations, and may further the same general goals, 
with which you were involved as a public official, will not, by 
itself, result in any conflict of interest under Section 
87400-87405. 
I hope this has answered your questions. 
If you have any 
further questions, please do not hesitate to call. 
Very truly yours, 
" 
I

Chief, Legal Division 
BAM:km 


August 31, 1983 
3 
Ms. Barbara Millman 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street Building 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear ~E. Millman: 
This letter is an inquiry to the Fair Political Practice Commission for your 
determination as to whether my intent to contract services with the California 
Department of Parks and Recreation (CDPR) is prohibited under Article 4 -
Disqualification of Former Officers and Employees, 87400-87405. 
I served at the pleasure of the CDPR Director as Assistant Director from 
September 1978 to January 1983 and resigned from the position on November 23, 1982 
accepted by Resources Secretary on January 5, 1983. 
Part of my function in 
this position was to facilitate accessability of CDPR facilities for population 
groups that heretofore had not used such facilities, i.e. Hispanic, Blacks, 
Seniors, Disabled and Native Americans. 
This was accomplished by developing 
pilot programs targetted to specific population groups as well as through 
serving as Executive Secretary to CDPR Statewide Citizen Advisory Councils, 
which provided input to CDPR policy decisions. 
Currently I have before the CDPR, National Park Service (NPS) and the California 
Department of Education (DOE), a proposal to facilitate accessibility for the 
Hispanic community. 
The proposal calls for a cooperative effort between the 
NPS, DOE, and CDPR. 
The question then is; am I, under Article 4 - 87400 - 87402 
prohibited to enter into A contract for services with the CDPR? 
If ~eeded I car furnish additional information. 
please 
ear]iest convenience. 
Thank you for your attention. 
I 
! 
Sin1erelYr 
I 
; 
!

Street 
Davis, CA 
95616

respond at your

sections.question: You have asked whether, as a former official of the Department of Parks and Recreation, you may enter into a contract with the Department in connection with a proposal to promote access to Department facilities for the Hispanic Community.
sections.conclusion: So long as you will not be paid for representing any individual or organization in connection with a particular project or proceeding in which you participated as a public official, Sections 87400-87405 are not applicable. The fact that your proposed activities may involve the same general policy considerations, and may further the same general goals, with which you were involved as a public official, will not, by itself, result in any conflict of interest under Section 87400-87405.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a former Department of Parks and Recreation official who worked on general planning for minority group access enter into a paid contract with the Department for a similar project under Government Code Sections 87400-87405?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The former official may enter into a contract with the Department because he was not involved in specific proceedings but only general planning and policy work. The restriction applies only to paid representation in particular matters or proceedings in which the official participated.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (dated August 31, 1983) and the FPPC response (dated September 12, 1983). Response letter clearly contains Question and Conclusion sections. Advice number A-83-203 matches document ID provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked whether, as a former official of the Department of Parks and Recreation, you may enter into a contract with the Department in connection with a proposal to promote access to Department facilities for the Hispanic Community.

CONCLUSION: So long as you will not be paid for representing any individual or organization in connection with a particular project or proceeding in which you participated as a public official, Sections 87400-87405 are not applicable. The fact that your proposed activities may involve the same general policy considerations, and may further the same general goals, with which you were involved as a public official, will not, by itself, result in any conflict of interest under Section 87400-87405.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to Ricardo Ramirez's inquiry about whether he may contract with the Department of Parks and Recreation to promote Hispanic community access to facilities. The FPPC concludes that since his former work involved only general planning (not specific proceedings), he is permitted to enter into the proposed contract.
